3beabaef-96bd-42c4-9c4d-ac686ae7c3c9.full_text: SHREEJIT MAGADUM +91 86187 29579 | shreejitmagadum2003@gmail.com | linkedin.com/in/shreejitm | github.com/Shreejit123-M PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY Detail-oriented ECE graduate with 11 months of administrative support experience and strong expertise in documentation, stakeholder coordination, and process compliance. Skilled in data flow analysis, risk assessment reporting, and privacy-aligned system design — directly transferable to Data Privacy and Data Governance frameworks. Combines a consulting mindset with technical depth to deliver structured, audit-ready outputs aligned with GDPR principles and Deloitte's privacy practice. CORE SKILLS Privacy & Compliance: Data Privacy, GDPR Concepts, PIA, Data Flow Mapping, ROPA, Gap Assessment, Data Governance, Data Inventory, Policy Drafting, Privacy Frameworks Analytical & Risk: Risk Assessment, Compliance Reporting, Data Classification, Cybersecurity Fundamentals, Threat Analysis Technical Tools: Python, SQL, Flask, FastAPI, AWS (EC2, S3, Lambda), Docker, Linux, Git, MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) Consulting Skills: Stakeholder Communication, SOP Documentation, Problem Solving, Attention to Detail, Client-Facing Presentation EXPERIENCE Keyence Corporation Feb 2024 – Dec 2024 Administrative Assistant | Bangalore, India | 11 months Managed end-to-end scheduling, meeting coordination, and travel itinerary planning for senior leads, maintaining zero scheduling conflicts across multiple concurrent projects. Authored and maintained SOPs, process documentation, and project trackers in Word, Excel, and Outlook — ensuring version-controlled, audit-ready records compliant with internal quality standards. Automated routine reporting workflows using Python scripts, reducing manual processing effort by 30% and improving weekly status report turnaround time significantly. Served as primary stakeholder liaison — coordinated queries across engineering, procurement, and vendor teams; drafted correspondence and escalated issues proactively to senior directors. Supported expense reconciliation, meeting minutes, and preparation of executive-ready PowerPoint presentations for senior director review and client-facing deliverables. KEY PROJECTS Corn Leaf Disease Detection — IEEE Published Research May 2025 TensorFlow, Keras, ResNet, Grad-CAM Built AI model governance framework with full evaluation suite (precision, recall, F1, confusion matrix) — mirroring privacy risk assessment and compliance validation workflows before production deployment. Applied Grad-CAM explainability to expose model decision logic, enabling transparent and auditable AI outputs aligned with Privacy by Design and data accountability principles. Authored IEEE peer-review standard documentation — demonstrating rigorous technical writing skills directly applicable to ROPA, PIA, and data protection policy deliverables. Custom Search Engine Jan 2026 Python, Flask, SQLite, TF-IDF, REST API Engineered a data indexing pipeline over 1,000+ documents with query caching — reflecting data inventory management, data classification, and privacy-aware system design practices. Designed RESTful API with data minimization principles in schema design to limit unnecessary data

4154fc09-7a6c-4923-b779-c3950467a0fd.title: Embedded Systems Assignment (chunk 1/2)
4154fc09-7a6c-4923-b779-c3950467a0fd.text_preview: Embedded System Engineer We are building a wearable AI assistant. It clips to a shirt, listens for a command, and talks to an LLM (Large Language Model) in the cloud. The Challenge: It must run on a tiny battery for 12 hours. Part 1: Hardware & Archi…
4154fc09-7a6c-4923-b779-c3950467a0fd.full_text: Embedded System Engineer We are building a wearable AI assistant. It clips to a shirt, listens for a command, and talks to an LLM (Large Language Model) in the cloud. The Challenge: It must run on a tiny battery for 12 hours. Part 1: Hardware & Architecture Goal: Prove you understand low-power design and audio processing. Question 1: The “Always-Listening” Problem To catch a wake word (like “Hey Computer”), the microphone must always be on. This drains the battery. Proposed Design: Using a dual-core SoC (System on Chip)  Core A: o High performance o Wi-Fi capable o Power hungry  Core B: o Low power o Slow speed o Limited RAM Your Task: Explain how you would split the work between Core A and Core B to maximize battery life.  Which core handles wake-word detection?  Which core handles cloud upload? Question 2: Memory Calculation (Audio Buffer) We need to record user audio before sending it to the cloud.  Format: o 16 kHz sample rate o 16-bit depth (2 bytes per sample) o Mono  Duration: 10 seconds  Constraint: The chip has 256 KB of SRAM available for this buffer. Task: Calculate the total size of the 10-second audio clip.  Will it fit in RAM?  Show your math. Question 3: Privacy Hardware Users are worried about being recorded secretly.  We want a “Hard Mute” switch that physically cuts microphone power. Schematic Check: If the software crashes, can it accidentally turn the mic back on?  How would you wire the switch to ensure absolute privacy? Part 2: Firmware Logic (C Code) Goal: Write a robust state machine for the device. The Scenario Write the C code that manages the device’s main states. You are writing the “Brain” of the pin. Mock Functions (Assume these exist) Mock Functions (Assume these exist)  bool is_button_pressed(void) Returns true if the user holds the touch bar.  int get_battery_level(void) Returns a value from 0 to 100.  void set_led_color(int color) Colors: OFF, GREEN (Listening), RED (Error).  void start_audio_stream(void) Starts sending audio to Wi-Fi.  void stop_audio_stream(void) Stops sending audio. The Logic Rules 1. IDLE State o Default state o LED is OFF o Audio is OFF 2. LISTENING State o Enter ONLY if:  Button is pressed AND  Battery > 10% o If battery < 10%, go to ERROR immediately o Turn LED GREEN o

34891662-b50c-40d5-b658-2ab3b83f9968.title: Embedded Systems Assignment (chunk 2/2)
34891662-b50c-40d5-b658-2ab3b83f9968.text_preview: int get_battery_level(void) Returns a value from 0 to 100.  void set_led_color(int color) Colors: OFF, GREEN (Listening), RED (Error).  void start_audio_stream(void) Starts sending audio to Wi-Fi.  void stop_audio_stream(void) Stops sending audio.…
34891662-b50c-40d5-b658-2ab3b83f9968.full_text: int get_battery_level(void) Returns a value from 0 to 100.  void set_led_color(int color) Colors: OFF, GREEN (Listening), RED (Error).  void start_audio_stream(void) Starts sending audio to Wi-Fi.  void stop_audio_stream(void) Stops sending audio. The Logic Rules 1. IDLE State o Default state o LED is OFF o Audio is OFF 2. LISTENING State o Enter ONLY if:  Button is pressed AND  Battery > 10% o If battery < 10%, go to ERROR immediately o Turn LED GREEN o Start Audio 3. Hold-to-Talk o Device stays in LISTENING only while the button is held 4. Finishing o When the button is released:  Stop Audio  Turn LED OFF  Return to IDLE 5. Safety o If the button is stuck (held for > 30 seconds):  Force a reset to IDLE  Prevent overheating Constraints  Use a switch statement for the state machine  Do not use floating point numbers  Write clean, readable code
